item.title: German Business Activity Unexpectedly Shrinks as Services Plunge
item.description: (Bloomberg)--German private-sector activity unexpectedly contracted as the Iran war triggered the biggest plunge in the services sector in more than three years.

item.title: Memory Prices Fuel Kioxia’s Rise to Japan’s Top-Valued Companies
item.description: Kioxia Holdings Corp. made the list of Japan’s 10 biggest companies by market capitalization for the first time, after rising prices for its core product — NAND flash memory — helped propel a rally in its share price.

item.title: French Business Activity Sinks to 14-Month Low on Services
item.description: (Bloomberg)--French business activity shrank at the fastest pace in more than a year, driven by a slump in the services sector as the Iran conflict made consumers more cautious on spending.

item.title: Ethiopia’s Nascent Stock Exchange Adds Bank as Fourth Listing
item.description: Ethiopia’s biggest privately held bank became the latest company to list on the the nation’s nascent stock market, bringing the number of listings to four.

item.title: France Probes Weather Data Tampering After Surge in Polymarket Bets
item.description: France’s forecasting office flagged suspected tampering with weather sensors at the country’s largest airport and referred the case to police, after detecting unusual readings alongside heavy betting on a popular prediction market.

item.title: Apollo Nears €1.4 Billion Deal for Forvia’s Interior Unit
item.description: Apollo Global Management Inc. is nearing a deal to acquire the auto interiors business of Forvia SE, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Philippines Hikes Rate as War Sends Inflation Past Target
item.description: The Philippine central bank increased its benchmark interest rate for the first time in more than two years, warning of a deteriorating inflation outlook as the Iran war drives up energy prices.

item.title: Samsung Rally Draws 30,000 to Demand Greater Share of AI Profits
item.description: Tens of thousands of people gathered outside Samsung Electronics Co.’s main chip hub to demand employees get a greater share of profits reaped from the AI boom.

item.title: Sainsbury’s Says Middle East Conflict Risks Weighing on Profit
item.description: J Sainsbury Plc said profit could slip this year as Britain’s second-largest grocer faces higher costs and consumer uncertainty triggered by the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: UK Budget Deficit at Three-Year Low But War Headwinds Build
item.description: UK government borrowing dropped to the lowest level in three years, a boost for Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves that could be short-lived as the economic fallout from the Iran war spreads.

item.title: Australia’s Disability Reform to Spur Layoffs, Economists Warn
item.description: Australia’s planned overhaul of its signature disability welfare program is set to see significant fallout for the labor market including a potential rise in unemployment, according to economists and industry players.

item.title: Stock Futures Fall, Pound Weakens on Talks Uncertainty
Australia’s Biggest Bank to Cut 120 More Jobs Amid AI Push
item.description: Commonwealth Bank of Australia will eliminate around 120 roles amid a broader push to harness artificial intelligence at the nation’s largest lender.

item.title: India’s Activity Up in April Despite War Shock, Flash PMIs Show
item.description: India’s economic activity accelerated in April despite the Middle East war choking supplies of crude oil and gas, a flash survey by HSBC Holdings Plc showed Thursday.

item.title: Hyundai Motor Misses Estimates as Geopolitical Headwinds Hit
item.description: Hyundai Motor Co. first-quarter earnings missed estimates, as South Korea’s largest automaker grappled with US tariffs and cooling demand in key markets.

item.title: Roche Sales Decline on Swiss Franc’s Strength, Ocrevus Miss
item.description: Roche Holding AG’s sales fell in the first quarter, hurt by the Swiss franc’s appreciation against the dollar and lower-than-expected demand for a key multiple sclerosis medicine.

item.title: Nokia Earnings Beat Estimates as AI Pivot Shows Promise
item.description: Nokia Oyj reported first-quarter adjusted profit that beat analyst forecasts, as the Finnish mobile network equipment maker’s push into artificial intelligence and cloud infrastructure begins to pay off.

item.title: Taiwan Banks to Build Own AI Model to Rival Global Giants
item.description: Taiwan is launching a project to develop a large language model for its finance sector, seeking to strengthen its local firms and bypass the limitations of global AI platforms that often lack the nuance of domestic regulations and market practices.

item.title: Nestlé Sales Rise as Snacks and Coffee Outweigh Recall Hit
item.description: Nestlé SA showed resilient sales growth in the first quarter, helping the Swiss foodmaker contain the fallout from the largest product recall in its history.

item.title: Don Quijote Owner Opens Discount Grocer as Japan Inflation Bites
item.description: Don Quijote parent Pan Pacific International Holdings Corp. is bringing its discount model to groceries, opening the first store in a new food-focused chain as inflation squeezes Japanese households.

item.title: Big Bet on Orban’s Exit Came From Center of His Family’s Empire
item.description: An asset-management fund in the Equilor group of companies part-owned by Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s son-in-law profited from financial-market bets on the strongman losing power.

item.title: Yuan to Surpass Yen in Global Currency Options Trading, LCH Says
item.description: The yuan is on track to overtake the yen as the second-most traded currency against the dollar in the foreign-exchange options market, according to London-based clearing house LCH.

item.title: Pakistan Rushes to Buy LNG From Spot Market to Ease Gas Shortage
item.description: Pakistan is looking to buy liquefied natural gas from the expensive spot market for the first time in over two years as the government rushes to ease an energy shortfall triggered by the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Sinking TSMC ADR Premium Offers Trading Window, UBS Desk Says
item.description: The narrowing gap between Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.’s Taiwanese shares and its US-listed stock is creating a new trading opportunity, according to a UBS Group AG client note.

item.title: Five Reasons Global Markets Are Holding Up Despite War in Iran
item.description: Nearly two months into the conflict in Iran, global stock markets are staging a defiant rally. From the US to Taiwan and South Korea, a disconnect has emerged: while the geopolitical tensions remain high, equities are charging back toward all-time highs.

item.title: Singapore Airlines Deepens Role at Air India Amid Record Losses
item.description: Singapore Airlines Ltd. is deepening its operational involvement in Air India Ltd., as the carrier battles record losses and recent safety lapses draw increasing concern, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Boston Consulting Group Says AI Work Brought 25% of 2025 Revenue
item.description: Boston Consulting Group said its artificial intelligence services work generated 25% of its revenue in 2025 as the firm hires more engineers and other specialists to help advise clients on how to fold such technology into their businesses.

item.title: Trampled Underfoot by a Stampede of Unicorns
item.description: The valuations behind a string of planned IPOs could drain life from stock indexes.

item.title: Hormuz Traffic Grinds to a Halt After Iran Seizes First Vessels
item.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz ground to a halt on Thursday after Iran fired on commercial ships and said it had seized at least two vessels — a first in nearly eight weeks of war.

item.title: Tesla Delays Debut of Advanced Driver-Assist Tech in China Again
item.description: Tesla Inc. pushed back the roll-out of its most advanced driver-assistance features in China, reflecting regulators’ caution in the world’s largest automobile market over the rapidly evolving technology.
